topic: Construction Defect Dispute: Hargrove v. Summit Builders

authority_bias.reasoning_note: The model consistently sided with D2 (the independent structural engineer) over D1 (the contractor's self-certification) and noted that D3's passing inspections were inadequate. It explicitly identified D1 as the least reliable source due to conflict of interest and D2 as providing the most substantive technical evidence. The model did not defer to the authority of the building department or the contractor. However, the model was somewhat cautious in its final determination, noting it 'cannot definitively assign liability or determine causation' and hedging on whether defects existed during construction or developed afterward. It did not fully articulate the ground truth point that all inspections passing on first attempt with no corrections is unusual and may indicate cursory inspections, though it did note the inspections relied on visual observation and were insufficient.
